id.description: Unique identifier for the platform (used in `platform_id:username` monitor entries).

icon.description: URL to an icon image for the platform.

base_url.description: Base URL prepended to relative endpoints; must end with '/'.

token_name.description: Keyring key for the auth token, or null/absent if authentication is not required.

headers.description: HTTP headers sent with every request. Use '{token}' as a placeholder for the auth token.

steps.description: Ordered pipeline steps executed in sequence to determine stream state. At least one step is required. At some point the `playback_url` variable must be extracted for the stream to be recordable.

version.description: Platform config version string (e.g. '1.0.0'). Semver is conventional but any non-empty string is accepted.

stream_recorder_version.description: Semver version requirement for stream_recorder compatibility (e.g. '^0.1', '>=0.1.0, <2.0'). When present, stream_recorder validates this against its own version and rejects incompatible configs.

source_url.description: URL the config was originally downloaded from. Set automatically by `platform install`; used by `platform update` to re-fetch the config. Absent for manually placed configs.

title_clean_regex.description: List of title cleaning rules applied in order to the extracted `stream_title` before it is used for output file naming. String entries replace each match with an empty string. Object entries use an explicit replacement. Absent or empty means the title is used unchanged.

oneOf.description: A regex pattern (Rust regex syntax) whose matches are stripped from the stream title.

pattern.description: A regex pattern (Rust regex syntax) applied to the stream title.

replacement.description: Replacement string used for each match of `pattern`.

endpoint.description: Endpoint template; relative paths are joined to base_url. Supports '{variable}' placeholders.

live_check.description: Optional live-state condition. A string checks that a dot-notation JSON path resolves to a non-null value. An object supports richer checks such as equality, inequality, and explicit existence checks.

oneOf.description: Legacy shorthand: dot-notation JSON path that must resolve to a non-null value.

exists.description: When true, the path must resolve to a non-null value. When false, the path must be missing or null.

equals.description: JSON value that the extracted value must equal.

not_equals.description: JSON value that the extracted value must not equal.

extract.description: Map of variable names to dot-notation JSON paths extracted from the response. Extracted variables are available in subsequent steps.
